wordmark.purpose: UnifrakturCook blackletter, logotype only. Forbidden in running text.

display.purpose: Cinzel section titles; cut-stone headlines that open each fold.

statement.purpose: Cinzel first-person declarations in the pitmaster's voice.

lede.purpose: Jost 400 intro paragraph under a heading, capped ~54ch.

body.purpose: Jost 300 running copy; line-height >= 1.6 on the dark ground.

label.purpose: Jost 600 uppercase brass kicker/eyebrow and field labels.

shadows.name: panel-sink
shadows.purpose: The offer/booking panel settling into the dark room; sits in, not above.

shadows.name: popover-lift
shadows.purpose: Calendar popover; deep near-black, never gray.

shadows.name: ember-commit-glow
shadows.purpose: Booking submit on hover; the only colored shadow, reads as heat thrown forward.

shadows.name: brass-hairline-glow
shadows.purpose: Under the gold dividers; faint gilt shimmer, not elevation.

motion.name: ease-signature
motion.purpose: The single shared easing curve (--ease) for all state transitions and reveals.

motion.name: entrance-fadeup
motion.purpose: Staggered hero entrance (kicker .2s, heading .35s, lede .5s, etc.).

motion.name: ken-burns
motion.purpose: Perpetual slow drift on every full-bleed image; one diagonal per tile.

motion.name: gold-shine
motion.purpose: Moving highlight sweeping the metallic dish dividers.

components.name: Primary Button (Booking Submit)
components.description: The one heavy, committed action; oxblood block that flares with ember glow on commit.
components.html: <button class="ds-submit">Send the request<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="18" height="18" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M5 12h14M13 6l6 6-6 6"/></svg></button>

components.name: Nav CTA
components.description: Quiet brass-outlined call to action in the nav; inverts to solid brass on hover.

components.name: Editorial Underline Field
components.description: Boxless underline input; the brass line catches fire (ember) on focus.

components.name: Guest Preset Chip
components.description: Pill preset for the guest-count stepper; brass hairline that warms on hover.

components.name: Nav Link (underline grow)
components.description: Uppercase tracked nav link with a brass underline that grows from 0 to full width on hover.

components.name: Catering Accordion Tile
components.description: Signature component: full-height image tile that expands on hover and reveals copy. Not a static card grid.
components.html: WeddingsThe long table

narrative.overview: This is the hush before a film begins. The room is dark, a single flame is the only light, and one figure stands in it who clearly knows exactly what he is doing. Every screen of The Grillfader holds that frame: near-black ground, fire as the source of warmth and motion, type cut from stone, and copy spoken in the first person by a man who answers to no one. The site is a single-page invitation, not a brochure. Restraint is the luxury signal, never ornament. Warmth is carried by ember glow, aged brass, and the human voice, never by softness, friendliness, or a warm-tinted body background. Imagery does the heavy lifting and the chrome recedes so the photograph and the headline can carry the fold.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black, fire-lit ground; ember and brass are the only warmth.
Cinzel cut-stone display against Jost light body; UnifrakturCook reserved for the logo.
Full-bleed imagery with perpetual Ken Burns drift; chrome recedes to let it lead.
Forged, deliberate controls: oxblood blocks, ember-glow on commit, hairline brass borders.
One idea per fold; long, paced scroll that ends at the booking.

rules.name: The Fire-Lit Rule
rules.body: The ground is always dark and warm-leaning; never a tinted near-white 'cream' background. 'Cream' is a text color thrown by candlelight, not a surface.

rules.name: The Ember-Is-Alive Rule
rules.body: Ember (#E8772E) marks only live or active state: focus, selection, the current count, a hover glow. It is never a decorative or resting fill.

rules.name: The Stone-and-Smoke Rule
rules.body: Cinzel is for things carved to last (names, headings, declarations, numbers). Jost is for things spoken aloud (running copy, lede, labels).

rules.name: The Light-Body Rule
rules.body: Body is Jost 300 on a dark ground, so line-height never drops below 1.6. Thin light type on black needs the air.

rules.name: The No-Gray-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows are near-black or fire-colored (ember/brass glow). A neutral gray shadow breaks the darkroom. Depth is the dark swallowing edges, not panels floating on white.

dos: Do keep the ground Char Black (#120D0C) or its tonal siblings. Warmth comes from ember, brass, and fire imagery, never from the background.
Do reserve Ember (#E8772E) for live/active state only (focus, selection, current count, commit glow).
Do set every heading and declaration in Cinzel, every line of running copy in Jost 300 with line-height >= 1.6.
Do lead with full-bleed imagery and let Ken Burns drift carry the motion; let the photograph be the design.
Do use deep near-black or fire-colored shadows for depth; layer tonally (ground -> panel -> recess).
Do point every fold at the booking, and when a section feels busy, remove an element and enlarge what remains.
donts: Don't introduce a cream/sand/beige background. 'Cream' is a text color here; a light body bg breaks the opening-scene frame.
Don't drift into the generic catering/restaurant template: stock-photo grids, 'welcome to our restaurant', smiling-staff heroes.
Don't go rustic-farmhouse BBQ: chalkboard fonts, kraft paper, distressed wood, mason jars, smokehouse cliches.
Don't import the loud SaaS/startup landing: gradient blobs, glassmorphism as decoration, tiny tracked eyebrows over every section, hero-metric templates, identical feature-card grids.
Don't clutter or over-decorate. Nothing may fight the cinematic restraint; one idea per fold.
Don't use gray drop shadows, pill-shaped primary buttons, side-stripe accent borders, or gradient text.
Don't set UnifrakturCook anywhere but the logo, or body copy in Cinzel.
